label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's work through this problem step by step. The user is asking which of the options is a requirement to be admitted to the Work-Study Program described in the given text. The options are: A) get some government assistance. B) show your financial need. C) perform well in your studies. D) possess computing techniques. First, I'll look through the provided information to find any requirements or prerequisites for joining the Work-Study Program. The text begins by outlining the program's purpose: to help students work part-time to ease cost pressures. It mentions that the program is suitable if students need more money to cover education-related costs. Looking at the "Advantages" section, there are points about work experience, money, and easy access. The "Easy access" part says, "Applying for the program is easy. There is no need for face-to-face interview. All the forms you need are online; just fill in the forms and email us; we accept no letter or phone application!" So this section tells us how to apply but doesn't specify what requirements are necessary to qualify. Then, there's a "Did you know?" section: "At the University of Ottawa, it is not necessary to receive government assistance to qualify for our Work-Study Program." This tells us that receiving government assistance is NOT a requirement for the program (which eliminates option A). The implication is that other factors might be considered, but the key point is that government assistance is not required. Now, looking at the options again: A) get some government assistance. (Eliminated based on the "Did you know?" section.) B) show your financial need. (This is strongly implied because the program is for students needing more money to cover education costs.) C) perform well in your studies. (This is not mentioned as a requirement in the text.) D) possess computing techniques. (While one of the job examples is a computing and network technician, this is just one job type, not a general requirement for the program.) The main requirement mentioned in the text is
